headline: FTAV’s Friday chart quiz
body_text:
The theme for this week’s chart quiz is percentages.

In case you don’t have weekend reading plans, Wikipedia has a section on whether it’s the norm in various languages to leave a space between the number and the percentage sign. In Russian and Dutch they’re told to but usually don’t, whereas in Spanish it’s muddled and in Czech it’s pointlessly complicated. Fans of national stereotyping will find a lot here to work with.

Anyway. Charts. Name all three, win a tee.

Email your guesses for what those three charts represent to alphaville@ft.com, putting QUIZ in the subject line. One correct entry will be drawn at random from all those received for the prize of an exclusive FTAV quiz-chart-winner T-shirt.

We usually name the contestants who have all the right answers. If you don’t want that to happen, let us know. It won’t affect your chances. The deadline for entries is midday Monday, London time, and the judge’s decision is final.
